entry.title: Re: New Features in SAP Subscription Billing (2023-05-15)

Hello the integration SB - CI also means that its possible to use SB as rating engine for usage and avoid SAP CC?
Thanks,

entry.title: Absence Quota Generation - PT60
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Can someone please suggest on the below requirement.

We have a requirement that whenever a user works overtime of 2 hours, the employee is eligible to take off for that 2 hours anytime in next 30 days.

For example, User X worked overtime of 8 hours on 01.11.2024, then post time evaluation, the user should get an absence quota balance with the validity date as 02.11.2024 + 30 days.

However, when the same user also worked overtime of 8 hours again on 02.11.2024, then another absence quota record to be created with 8 hours balance with the validity date as 03.11.2024+30 days, but should not summed up to the existing quota (validity 02.11.2024+30days).

I was able to generate only one absence quota with validity as record date + 30 days. However, we would like to have multiple absence quota records with validity dates based on the date of overtime worked.

Can someone please help me how to achieve this?

Many thanks in advance.

Regards,
Sai

entry.content:
DEAR EXPERTS

 CAN WE MAINTAIN HIERARCHIES IN OPEN ODS VIEW

PLEASE HELP

entry.title: customer Credit group is not automatically moving from XD03 to FD33
entry.content:
Hi,

While creating customer master data only for 1 region the customer Credit group is not automatically moving from XD03 to FD33. and for other region it is working fine. 

Please do any once noticed this error before if yes please confirm how to resolve this issue 

I hope if any one can help me on it.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Could Not performe a simple Excel export.
entry.content:
Dear Support : 

when we select the following informations, 

 

FBL3N - Accounts: 63000000 -73999999
Formatting /AZZAB2

we cannot export them to excel, we have this the error "XSLT: No valid xml source. ", like if the system confuse between excel and xml the moment of the export: 
we cannot export to excel with the error : invalid xml source. 

Do you have any suggestions about this issue? 

Regards,

entry.title: System view not showing data in Container
entry.content:
Hello, 
When we are trying to access the system view (for example, _SYS_TASK. START_TASK_MESSAGES) as a synonym in the container project, it executes without any error but shows no data. 

Same view when accessed from the database SQL console using db user, it shows the data.

Is there any workaround we have to do to make this data visible from container?

Steps to replicate the issue. 

  1. Create a synonym on top of SYS_TASK.START_TASK_MESSAGES. 
  2. Do data preview on the synonym using the container sql console(RT user).  [Data will be not visible]
  3. Do SELECT * FROM _SYS_TASK.START_MESSAGES using your DB user [You will see the data].

System views which are having same issues are as follows.

  1.     SYS.M_TASKS
  2.     SYS.M_TABLE_STATISTICS
  3.     _SYS_TASK.START_TASK_MESSAGES
  4.     SYS. USER_PARAMETERS

Does anyone faced the same issue? Is there any workaround for this?

Thank you in advance.

Best Regards,

Ajinkya Jadhav

entry.title: Re: Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide
entry.content:
Hi @tongzheng,

The Vector DB is not at the customer BTP Account; it's a tenant managed by the product team, created during the service creation. These tenants has tenant isolation and security in place.

Regards,

Nagesh

entry.title: Re: Elimination Balance Sheet and P&L
entry.content:
• Group Reporting (GR) always stores periodic (PER) data regardless of how data was selected or loaded (YTD or PER). There are "conversion capabilities" at various places that support conversion of YTD <--> PER (e.g. file upload, analytics, etc.). 
  • When loading external data users can chose PER or YTD. A general recommendation is to load YTD to make sure YTD data is consistent between source and GR. System will calculate the delta for the processing period by deducting the already existing YTD of the processing period. This leads to a PER amount in GR stored in the processing period.
  • In analytics YTD is calculated by the system selecting periods 000-reporting period.
  • Eliminations can be configured with reclassification rules today. It can be configured how data is selected (YTD or PER). Hence BS can be configured YTD, PL can be configured PER. Same applies to currency translation.

entry.title: Fiori Elements odata V4 List Report missing option to select all columns
entry.content:
Dear community,

we noticed that in Fiori Elements List Reports built with oData V4 - both standard and custom ones, the option to "select all" columns in the table view setting dialog is missing (replaced by de-select all). Is it possible to adjust this either by any manifest settings or annotations? I couldn't find any resource for this.

In oData V2 List Report the "select all" checkbox is available in the view settings dialog.

entry.title: Re: SOIN -EXCEL data Binary conversion issue - S050 method - PROCESS_INBOUND - cont_hex[]
entry.content: i am working on ECC 6.0 SAP BASIS 700 SP Level 0030 . i dont find XLSX file via the Excel XCO interface or abap2xlsx in system. i will let you know if any i find any solution . Thank you for your replies.

entry.title: Re: Parallel insert requests in CAP
entry.content: Can you please provide more details of your setup? E.g. your subaccount ID, BTP region and your instance ID. Please notice, that the performance of your instance depends on the choosen configuration. Both the CPU and the disk have quotas, which you could hit by running this kind of tests.

entry.title: Re: Error in learning journey content / designing asset accounting
entry.content: Hi @PaulW1 Thank you for highlighting. I forwarded your inquiry to our SAP experts who will analyses the problem, and will revert back to you once the issue is solved.

entry.title: SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 01 - History of SAP Extended Warehouse Management
entry.content:
Welcome to our blog post for the SAP EWM Podcast Episode 01 – The history of SAP EWM.

You can listen to the podcast episode in English and in German on Spotify, Amazon Music, Apple Podcasts or Sway! Enjoy!

A look back to the 90s!

Warehouse management is not a new topic at SAP. Already in 1991 SAP released LES-WM as part of SAP R/3. At the beginning of 2002 SAP decided to develop a new warehouse management product - SAP Extended Warehouse Management. There were several reasons for the new development for example increased performance requirements, a more flexible process design, the direct integration of material flow systems or mobile working capabilities.

In 2005 SAP published the first release of SAP EWM. And since 2005 SAP delivers continously new functions and innovations for EWM to its customers. That means SAP develops warehouse management solutions for more than 30 Years now. 

The technical foundation of SAP EWM has changed several times in the past. At the beginning it was implemented on the Supply Chain Management platform, later – with EWM 9.0 - it was also possible to run it as a standalone solution on the Netweaver stack. Usually always as a decentralized system.

Warehouse Management with SAP - More than 30 years of experience

 

The move to SAP S/4HANA

In 2015  we launched SAP S/4HANA – our new ERP flagship and the successor of SAP ECC 6.0

And since 2016 EWM is available on SAP S/4HANA. With SAP S/4HANA release 1610 and 1709 SAP EWM was only available as an integrated warehouse management solution - the so-called embedded EWM in S / 4HANA. Because it was very clear that a state-of-the-art warehouse management solution should be integral part of SAP S/4HANA. With the release 1809 in the year 2018, the decentralized deployment option for EWM on S/4HANA was launched too.

Today customers have the option to use EWM either within an S/4HANA system or still as a decentralized system - either connected to an SAP ECC or a SAP S/4HANA system via native SAP to SAP integration. The decision whether embedded or decentralized is the better choice for a customer depends on various aspects - functional, operational and strategic aspects. Our podcast episode 2 is about this decision. 

Options for inventory management and warehouse management with SAPBasic vs. advanced Warehousing

 

For embedded EWM in S/4HANA there are also two operating models: Basic and advanced warehousing. Basic warehousing describes the license-free option comparable with LE-WM in SAP ECC. It's from a commercial perspective part of SAP S/4HANA and offers already features such as multi-level processes, handling unit management or the complete mobile working environment.

Advanced warehousing allows customers to use the full range of EWM functions to run and optimize their warehouses. It offers for example an integrated material flow control, a highly flexible wave management, kitting and value added services, work in process handling and many more. Advanced warehousing is a "best of breed" warehouse management solution combined with SAP's "best of suite" offering. 

The operating model basic warehousing is only available with embedded EWM in S/4HANA. And customers can choose on warehouse number level if a warehouse runs with basic or advanced warehousing. A decentralized EWM on S/4HANA however is always "advanced warehousing". From a source code perspective, there is no difference between basic and advanced warehousing. The complete source code is always available.

High Level Overview Basic vs. advanced Warehousing

 

The future of warehouse management with SAP

SAP EWM  on S/4HANA is the successor of SAP LE-WM. The mainstream maintenance for LE-WM as part of SAP ECC will end at the latest by end of 2027. There are different options to move forward with existing LE-WM Implementations. 

As part of an SAP S/4HANA migration customers can continue to use LE-WM in SAP S/4HANA as so called compatibility scope until end of 2025 for example to migrate other modules first or not migrate all warehouses to EWM at once.

There is also option of Stock Room Management which is part of SAP S/4HANA and which can be used in S/4HANA also after 2025. From a source code perspective it is LE-WM however some functionalities such as the decentralized WM or the material flow connection (LE-WCU Interface) are no longer part of Stock Room Management. Stock Room Management enables customers to run LE-WM implementations for simple warehouses without any adjustments also in S/4HANA. There is a report which supports customers to check if existing LE-WM implementations are Stock Room Management compliant that means that the implementations do not use any features which are not covered by the usage rights of Stock Room Management. It also important to mention that no further developments are planned for Stock Room Management. 

Stock Room Management as a bridging technology on the way to SAP EWM

 

And therefore it makes sense to discuss the migration of all LE-WM implementations to EWM. For new warehouse implementations SAP EWM is recommended. With embedded EWM SAP offers an integrated warehouse management solution in SAP S/4HANA that meets the latest logistics requirements. It is seamlessly integrated in S/4HANA. A very good example are the synchronous postings of goods receipt for production directly onto SAP EWM storage bins with the well-know transaction MIGO. In addition, users benefit from the intuitive SAP FIORI strategy and warehouses can be managed based on real-time KPIs defined with capabilities of embedded analytics.

SAP EWM - A holistic and integrated intralogistics platform

 

SAP EWM offers customers the best of both worlds - a best of breed WMS which is natively integrated in to SAP's solution portfolio in a best of suite approach. 

Helpful Links: 

SAP S/4HANA Feature Scope Description (basic vs. advanced warehousing)
SAP Note 2881166 - FAQ Stock Room Management
SAP Note 2577428 - Roadmap for LE-WM in S/4HANA

Please visit our SAP EWM Podcast Landing Page with all Episodes, Blog Posts and Podcast Notes!

entry.title: Re: can I use CORRESPONDING # for deep internal tables?
entry.content:
I think you can use CORRESPONDING with MAPPING. Have you tried it?

Here is ABAP documentation link and example code from it:

DATA: BEGIN OF struct1,
        mcomp1 TYPE i VALUE 1,
        mcomp2 TYPE i VALUE 2,
        BEGIN OF substruc,
          subcomp1 TYPE i VALUE 1,
          subcomp2 TYPE i VALUE 2,
          subcomp3 TYPE i VALUE 3,
        END OF substruc,
      END OF struct1.

DATA: BEGIN OF struct2,
        comp2 TYPE i,
        comp1 TYPE i,
        BEGIN OF substruc,
          comp3 TYPE i,
          comp2 TYPE i,
          comp1 TYPE i,
        END OF substruc,
      END OF struct2.

struct2 =
  CORRESPONDING #(
    struct1 MAPPING comp1    = mcomp1
                    comp2    = mcomp2
                  ( substruc = substruc MAPPING comp1 = subcomp1
                                                comp2 = subcomp2
                                                comp3 = subcomp3 ) ).

entry.title: Do we need SAP PLM to integrate Teamcenter PLM
entry.content:
We need to integrate the Teamcenter with SAP. But I have some queries. Please input ideas.

1. Do we need SAP PLM to integrate the Teamcenter PLM or it's not required?

2. Can Teamcenter PLM be used to create the ECN and transfer it to SAP?

3. Can Teamcenter PLM master data be transferred directly to PEO? What and all data will be transferred (Material, BOM, ECN...).

4. Do we need to write BAPI codes in SAP to map the fields and to create master data?

5. Can Routing be configured in PLM?

6. If we use ECN in Teamcenter PLM then what can be the advantage of PEO? All BOM version changes can be done in PLM itself right? Why do we need to do some version changes in SAP PEO?

7. If new materials are created in Teamcenter PLM will they be created in SAP by integration automatically or need manual intervention?

entry.title: Don't Miss Out: Explore Key Improvements in SAP F&R with Collective Note 2335048
entry.content:
Dear SAP Forecasting and Replenishment  community,

Do you know already note 2335048 - F&R Improvements and Recommendations? If not, you are really missing out on important news! This note is a so called ‘collective note’ – it contains references to many other notes and all of them have one thing in common: they are no bug fixes but contain improvements, some smaller, some bigger! The note is updated on a regular basis with newly released relevant notes that include interesting new features.

Let’s have a look at some highlights of the recent improvements in more detail:

New Replenishment Types for Improving Multi-Echelon Replenishment Processes

There are 2 new replenishment types, 15 and 16. They behave very similarly like the ‘standard’ SAP F&R replenishment type 11 from the calculation logic but have some special twists:

  • RP type 15 can be used for products where a store order forecast is wanted for multi-echelon aggregation purposes, but no active order proposals should be generated for them with SAP F&R. So even if this might not be ideal (since the ‘real’ replenishment and store orders are done elsewhere and might differ quantity-wise), it is now possible to include these future demands for an aggregated store order forecast as a demand.
  • RP type 16 can be used to consider location products that are not ordered because of substitution locks in forecast and order forecast calculations. As a result, these numbers will also be considered by the aggregated order forecast calculation.

You can find more information in note 3415985 and its related notes.

Stock Projection can include Order Forecast

To understand the current and future stock of a location product the ‘Time Series Screen’ in SAP F&R is heavily used. It is part of the transaction ‘Cause Determination’ and is also embedded in the Replenishment Workbench and Product Workbench.  With note 3266842 you can now use as additional calculation basis options for the stock projection ‘Order Forecast’ and ‘Order Forecast with variable reorder point’ to include also potential requirements of future orders.

Additional Currencies When Working with the Replenishment Workbench

There is a so-called client currency in SAP F&R that is used when displaying and working with certain currency related values. In particular, when working with the Replenishment Workbench with different suppliers however, it might be useful to see some numbers in other currencies as well. With note 3117145 you can now define a user specific currency that is applied to the purchase and sales price fields in the navigation tree and the Order Proposal Item view as well. In addition to a user specific currency, you can specify a currency that is considered by the vendor restriction check. Check out the note for more details and how to set up variants that fit your needs. Note 3156854 helps you to work with selection variants that include additional currencies also in transaction /FRE/RWB_ORDNO (Single Order Proposal display).

Better Traceability with the Quick Overview

Sometimes it might be necessary to dig really into the details of an order proposal line item quantity. Even though there is already a lot of information on the line item view itself, this might not be sufficient to explain the result. This is where the so called ‘Quick Overview’ can help. Even though this comprehensive screen that is accessible out of the Replenishment or Product Workbench is available since 2020, it is important to know that it was continuously enhanced recently and covers now also ‘special‘ situations. If you do not know this useful screen please check it out -you will like it. No side-by-side excel calculations anymore – all important numbers are in one screen, divided by stock and demand period. Note  2862224 is the initial note that introduces the screen and there are multiple others for enhancements, e.g. 3422481 Quick Overview: Support of Requirements Calculation without a Stock Period 3489495 - Quick Overview - Optimization Quantities – TLO or Note 3214101 - Quick Overview: Safety Amount - Integration of Percentage Safety Amount for AOF.  All of these and more are also listed in the general collective note 2335048.

Order Forecast Improvements

Also not brand new but important to mention are these order forecast related notes: 2854849 - Order Forecast: Activation of time-dependent Unit of Measure and Reorder / Target Stock and 3292755 - MER: New option for determination of right border in AOF calculation.

  • By implementing enhancements /FRE/FU_FRP_BADI020 and /FRE/FU_FRP_BADI021), it is possible with the first note to consider time-dependent values for reorder point and target stock or different units of measure. As a result, more accuracy can be achieved.
  • Note 3292755 deals with the issue that a too short time horizon has been defined for some location product combinations, so that during the aggregation of the order forecast, this limits the entire data set. With this note you can now choose between an aligned horizon that considers this border and a maximum horizon that aggregates independently of what is available. This means that the result might not be consistent but since there were discussions to consider it nonetheless instead of nothing, this is now possible to choose!

This is just a glimpse of what has been done recently and also in the past years and I encourage you to browse through note 2335048 - F&R Improvements and Recommendations. I am sure you will find also other valuable points that help you improve your SAP Forecasting and Replenishment implementation.

Happy reading and kudos to our great support team!

if user is still need that the requirement you have given, then its a false requirement and ask them to remove the hold button itself.

now to remove hold button see the blog below 

 

Deactivating Hold Functionality in Purchase order Screen

kUnal

entry.title: How to update the PO Sender email from Network to a custom email ID
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We have a requirement to update the PO sender email ID from standard email ordersender-prod@ansmtp.ariba.com to custom email ID. Is this possible at Business network level? The client wants the Email ID to be their own custom email ID and not the standard email ID when emails are triggered from Business network to Suppliers for all transactions. 

Has anyone worked on customizing the Email ID at Network level or is this possible at all?

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: Error Loading 0FI_GL_4 Datasource to SAP Datasphere: "Disable Delta Capturing" Requirement
entry.content:
Dear experts 

I am trying to load the 0FI_GL_4 datasource from SAP S/4HANA into SAP Datasphere. However, I am encountering the following error:

 

Spoiler
To replicate an object that does not have a primary key using source connection type ABAP, you must first disable delta capturing for this table.

Is there a way to configure 0FI_GL_4 to work with delta capturing in Datasphere by adding a primary key or using a custom solution.

Priyanka

entry.title: Where to view tickets that have been de-escalateded?
entry.content:
I have a question for your help.

Case：After Level 1 agent receives the customer's ticket, L1 agent finds that he can't solve it, so L1 agent escalate the ticket to L2. L2 agent can't solve it, so L2 agent escalate the ticket to L3. After L3 provides a solution, de-escalate the ticket to L2/ L1.

My problem: Tickets that have been de-escalateded by L3 people will no longer be able to appear in “My Tickets” page. If L3 wants to see tickets that have been de-escalateded to L1/L2, how can they find them？

Looking forward to your reply, thank you in advance！

entry.title: SAP Identity Management Eclipse plugin 8.8.45 not available on tools.hana.ondemand.com
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I noticed that the Eclipse plugin for SAP Identity Management, which has so far been publicly available from SAP's official Eclipse software site at https://tools.hana.ondemand.com has not been updated there for a while.

The latest Eclipse plugin version available from the public site is 8.8.41. In SAP Service Marketplace, however, the latest version is 8.8.45 already.

Is this a bug or has there been an announcement that the public download channel will be abandoned?

SAP Identity Management

entry.title: SAC - Content Usage (Open Story) per month
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I'd like to know how often a story is accessed throughout the year in every month. But I cannot find any chart/table in system overview showing it. All I get is a cumulative figure for the selected time horizon in System - Usage section.

Is there somewhere such overview? Or any guidance how to create it?

Thanks and best regards
Axel

entry.title: Where can start learning how to utilize SAP Business AI and Generative AI?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Boosting your Cloud Transformation Journey with SAP Business AI and Generative AI learning journey.


Overview
The course is designed to empower learners with the knowledge and skills required to leverage SAP Business AI and Generative AI within their cloud transformation initiatives.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, learners will be able to effectively utilize SAP Business AI and Generative AI to enhance their cloud transformation journey. They will understand the strategic positioning and benefits of SAP Business AI, comprehend the role of Generative AI in process optimization and IT portfolio management, and gain the skills to build integrations and AI applications.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SF Learning - Imported OCN Items Survey
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Would it be possible to associate a survey template to every all OCN items so that we won't have to associate it manually every time a new OCN content has been imported?

entry.title: Re: Tables and Fields for Stock - Multiple Materials modu...
entry.content:
As I know:

1. Reporting Date: Derived from system or document date in the MKPF table's BUDAT field.
2. Sales Order: Stored in the VBAK table's VBELN field.
3. Shelf Life Expiration Date: Stored in the MCHB table's VFDAT field.
4. Supplier Name: Stored in the LFA1 table's NAME1 field.
5. Customer: Stored in the KNA1 table's NAME1 field.

These are standard SAP tables and fields. Your system's actual tables and fields may differ.

entry.title: Re: cannot create new report in LMS
entry.content: @BromikosHarry: The PRD is connected to the development instance of SFSF LMS. On the admin page for reports you will find the download option at the top of the page.

entry.title: Re: What port is used when I perform import in HANA DB?
entry.content: The SQL port of your HANA installation should be enough for the import actions. The default SQL port for HANA is 3<instance number>15. For example, if your instance number is 00, the SQL port would be 30015. However, it's always best to confirm with your specific HANA installation documentation as port numbers can be customized during installation.

entry.title: [PoC] Critical Authentication Vulnerability in SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform
entry.content:
On August 13, 2024, SAP released a crucial security update to address a severe authentication vulnerability identified in the SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform. This post provides an overview of the vulnerability, its potential impacts, and recommended mitigation steps to ensure your systems remain secure.

Vulnerability Overview

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2024-41730, has been assigned a critical CVSS v3.0 base score of 9.8. It specifically affects the Single Sign-On (SSO) functionality within the Enterprise authentication module of the SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform. The issue arises from a missing authentication check, which allows unauthorized attackers to exploit a REST endpoint to obtain a logon token. This could lead to a full system compromise, exposing sensitive business intelligence data, enabling manipulation of reports and data, and potentially disrupting BI services.

Affected Versions

The vulnerability impacts the following versions of the SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform:

  • ENTERPRISE 430
  • ENTERPRISE 440

    Proof of Concept

    A demonstration of how the vulnerability can be exploited is shown below:

     
    GET /biprws/logon/trusted HTTP/1.1
Host: SAP_BOBJ:6405
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:132.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/132.0
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8
Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.5
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate, br
Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1
Priority: u=0, i
X-SAP-TRUSTED-USER: administrator
     

    Mitigation and Patch Information

    SAP has promptly released patches to mitigate this vulnerability, ensuring that the Single Sign-On Enterprise authentication is secured by default once the patches are applied. The patches are available for the following versions:

    • SBOP BI PLATFORM SERVERS 4.3 – Patch Level SP005
    • SBOP BI PLATFORM SERVERS 2025 – Patch Level SP00
    • SBOP BI PLATFORM SERVERS 4.3 – Patch Level SP004

Organizations are strongly advised to apply these patches immediately to protect their systems from potential exploitation.

Recommendations

To safeguard your SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence environment, start by identifying all instances of the platform within your organization. Prioritize the deployment of the necessary patches based on the criticality of each affected system. Additionally, conduct a comprehensive security assessment of your BI environment to detect any signs of compromise. Enhancing your authentication mechanisms and access controls, along with vigilant monitoring of system logs for unusual activities related to authentication and token generation, will further strengthen your defenses against potential threats.

Conclusion

The identification of CVE-2024-41730 underscores the critical need for robust security practices within business intelligence platforms. Organizations utilizing the SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform must treat this vulnerability with the highest priority and implement the recommended patches without delay to protect their essential business data and operations.

Original Post: https://redrays.io/blog/critical-sap-businessobjects-authentication-vulnerability-cve-2024-41730/

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay Eligibility Fields in Design Worksheet
entry.content:
Okay, I did some further testing. I removed the column, and it had no impact on eligibility at all, so I still don't understand why it was added. Nevertheless, with the Manager Eligibility Rules, I managed to achieve the following: an employee is excluded from the EH processing but included in a worksheet. No data is displayed other than the name. It's a pity that the row is not grayed out, as it is in the Compensation worksheet.

This person falls into the second business rule:

Apologies if this seems like a basic post for the community, but I'm still exploring the Variable Pay capabilities. 😊 Hopefully, other newbies will find it helpful!

Xavier, again thank you for your help! As always - priceless!

entry.title: Re: System view not showing data in Container
entry.content: The problem may be due to user permissions in the container. Ensure the user has the required SELECT privileges on system views. If not, grant them using the GRANT statement, e.g., GRANT SELECT ON _SYS_TASK.START_TASK_MESSAGES TO <user>. Retry accessing system views. If the issue continues, contact SAP Support as it may be a SAP HANA bug.

entry.title: Payment reference
entry.content:
Hello,

when trying to post a AR document I receive the following error message. Our company is in Germany, the vendor we want to post to is in DK.

This is the message we receive: "Payment reference INV1234 is not correct for country Denmark"

Is it possible to change this message or setting?

Regards

Desiree

entry.title: Re: Using the BAPI_CREATE_SHIPMENT
entry.content:
If you're having trouble locating the shipment with the provided number, consider following these steps:

1. Remember to call 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT' after using the BAPI. This action will save any changes you've made. If you forget to do this, the shipment will be created, but it won't be saved.

2. Make sure you're looking in the correct transaction. For instance, VT03N is the transaction for creating a shipment.

3. Ensure you're looking in the correct system and client where the BAPI was executed.

4. Even if you received a success message, there might be warnings or errors that are preventing the shipment from being saved. Check the return table of the BAPI for any messages.

5. The shipment number could be within a range that isn't displayed in your transaction. Verify the number range for shipments in your system.

6. Make sure the data you've entered into the BAPI is correct. If it's not, this could be preventing the shipment from being saved.

7. If you've tried everything else and are still having issues, you might need to debug the BAPI to find the problem.

entry.title: How to scrape images from Google
entry.content:
Someone asked me how to scrape images from Google. I took it as a challenge.

Note that I did an image search, and took the Base64 versions of the images from the search page, which is how Google displays them. These are small, maybe 300-pixels square. If you wanted larger images, you'd extend the automation to click on the image to open the side panel, and use the right-click menu to save/download the image (my next challenge 😺). 

  1. Open Google.
  2. Enter your keyword in the search bar (the keyword comes from an input parameter).
  3. Click somewhere to get rid of the dropdown that hides the search button.
  4. Click "Google Search".
  5. Now on the results page, click "Images".
  6. Wait – before I put this 1s wait, my automation was quite unstable, sometimes worked, sometimes didn't. I believe not all the elements were in place at the beginning so waiting made sure they were loaded.
  7.  
  8. Iterate over all the pictures. I declared as an element anything with class = YQ4gaf – this was contained in the img tags.
  9. I retrieved the src property for the img tag – which was the image in Base64.
  10. I decoded the base64.
  11. I wrote the file to the path that was sent as an input parameter.
  12. Finally I set a condition so I would only loop over the first 10 images (well, 11 since I set the condition wrong 😮.

I then created a process with a trigger form with 2 inputs, the keyword for the Google search and the local path to store the pictures. But I could almost as easily create a Automation Launcher and User Task so I could trigger it attended whenever I wanted. 

 

How I Put It Together

Above looks like I knew what I was doing. But it took a little while, especially the following parts:

  • Recording the screens – this was BY FAR the most complicated part for me. I've tried the tutorials here and here, and hey are great, but I am still really not sure of myself on how the recording of screens works and the selecting of elements. Bu I'm getting there. I will work on it and create some videos on the opic.
  • Decoding Base64 – I was not sure how this was done, since we have documentation on using ctx library to do encoding/decoding, but I soon figured out how to use the Decode activity. I was already a little familiar with Base64 from SAP Build Apps, for which I created a blog on handling images, including in Base64 format.
  • Simulating Enter Key: This I did not even try. I would have needed it because when you enter a keyword the "Google Search" button is hidden, but online people said this was an issue. Instead, I simply clicked the Google logo to get rid of the dropdown that hid the button, and then used a click activity on the search button. 

 

Recording Screens

I did a recording by opening up Google in a new browser, and selecting Create → Application and selecting Recorder (not manual capture) and it created 2 screens – the Google home page, and then the results page.

On the results page, I created 2 captures. The main results page, and then the results page for images.

I also declared some elements I needed, like the search box and button. Most importantly, I declared the list of images, using the class as a criteria, and setting this to a collection.

 

Automation and Process

The automation is created for you, based on the recording, but I found there were all kinds of extra artifacts that I needed to delete or combine. Not a big deal, just something you have to do.

And then I had to add the part where we iterate through the images, decode the Base64 strings, save them to a file, and add a condition to just take the first 10 images. All using simple activities. I also created an input parameter for the keyword, instead of the hard-coded "cat" I used in the recording.

I created the artifacts for enabling this as an attended automation, creating:

  • Automation Launcher
  • User task (for entering the keyword and path)

The automation launcher you would have to register in the Control Tower for your environment.

And the user task you would have to add to your automation. Here is an example from my SAP CodeJam demo of sending emails using an automation.

entry.title: Re: S4 Public Cloud Development : "Logon to the ABAP serv...
entry.content: @SreekathKrishn Hello, I am facing the same issue when i are trying to connect s/4 hana private cloud system to eclipse, could you please help what needs to be done to resolve this issue?

entry.title: Re: SF Learning - Imported OCN Items Survey
entry.content:
@J4DENextCare Urgent Care wrote:

Hi Experts,

Would it be possible to associate a survey template to every all OCN items so that we won't have to associate it manually every time a new OCN content has been imported?

Certainly! Yes, it's possible to automate the association of a survey template to all OCN items so that it’s done automatically every time new OCN content is imported. To achieve this, you could set up an automated workflow or script that triggers the template association as part of the import process.

This could be done by:

  1. Modifying the import process: If you have control over how OCN items are imported, you could build a step into the import process that automatically assigns the predefined survey template to each new item.

  2. Using an API or custom script: If your system supports it, you could create a script that runs whenever new content is added, which then links the relevant survey template to the OCN items based on certain criteria (like item type or category).

  3. Updating system defaults: In some systems, you may be able to define a default survey template for specific content types. If that option is available, you could set up the template as the default for new OCN items so that it’s automatically linked during import.

  4. Custom workflows or triggers: If your platform supports custom workflows or triggers (like in content management or learning management systems), you can set up a rule that automatically associates the survey template whenever an OCN item is created or imported.

This way, the manual efforts of linking the survey template can be eliminated, streamlining the process and reducing the chance of errors or oversight.

entry.title: Form not shown in MyInbox Workzone standard
entry.content: In my workzone standard portal the My Inbox does show the tasks. But when I click on the task the form is not shown to approve or descline a task (see first screenshot). The inbox is working directly in the SAP BTP cockpit (second screenshot) so it seems a kind of authorization problem.

entry.title: SAP DRC & S/4HANA: Do I also need Ariba Central Invoice Mgmt. to process a supplier invoice to MIRO?
entry.content:
We want to connect the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (Cloud Edition) to S/4HANA (on Premise) for the implementation of E-Invoicing.

When we receive a supplier invoice via DRC: Can it automatically be transferred to the S/4HANA system and processed with proposal values for a MIRO posting?

Or do we need the Ariba Central Invoice Management as an additional tool?

 

Thank you & best regards

Janni

entry.title: Re: Using the capture screen in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
See my blog about how I did this: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/how-to-scrape-images-from-google/ba-p/13941824

Let me know what you think.

😺

entry.title: Re: FCV In Bank Accounts is Incorrect comparing to Trial...
entry.content:
The issue listen related to the Foreign Currency Valuation (FCV) in SAP, which adjusts the foreign currency values in your General Ledger (GL) accounts based on exchange rate differences.

Here's a step-by-step guide how possibly resolve it:

1. Make sure it's accurate and updated in the system. Navigate to SPRO -> SAP NetWeaver -> General Settings -> Currencies -> Enter Exchange Rates.

2. This is what determines how exchange rate differences are calculated. Navigate to SPRO -> Financial Accounting (New) -> General Ledger Accounting (New) -> Periodic Processing -> Valuate -> Define Valuation Methods.

3. After confirming the exchange rate and valuation method, execute the FCV again. Navigate to SAP Menu -> Accounting -> Financial Accounting -> General Ledger -> Periodic Processing -> Closing -> Valuate -> Foreign Currency Valuation.

4. Make sure your bank account's GL account is correctly set up as a balance sheet account with '10 - Company Code Currency' as the currency type.

5. Make sure the FCV configuration is correct. Navigate to SPRO -> Financial Accounting (New) -> General Ledger Accounting (New) -> Periodic Processing -> Valuate -> Prepare Automatic Postings for Foreign Currency Valuation.

If the problem continues after following these steps, it may be beneficial to reach out to a SAP Support Team.

entry.title: Re: Payment reference
entry.content:
Check 2506340 - Payment reference validation (KIDNO) for Denmark (F0 497)

Basically, use Customizing OBA5 to convert it to a warning  message (if you cannot, old version, first add it first in table T100S as an error message with SM30 as described in the note)

entry.title: Extend Datasphere Business content package.
entry.content:
Hello,
In BW you could make a copy of the Business content to extend it with more fields.
In the case of SAP Datasphere, what would be the recommendation? If we want to use the Sales package of the Business content, what would be the best practice to extend it?
I understand that if we extend the contents of the package directly, we would lose the changes in the case of a new import, right?
Thank you very much.

entry.title: Re: Payment reference
entry.content: Thank you for your reply - however we are in Public Cloud Version and I cannot access OBA5.

entry.title: Re: Most commonly used SAP CRM Tables, BDOCS and BADIs.

entry.title: Re: Update/Create the contract price for many price condi...
entry.content: May you can try to use VK12 to update SAP condition records.

entry.title: Re: Convert PNG/JPG attachment to base64 and send it via...
entry.content:
Hello Diana, thanks for your message, it helps us analyze the problem. The solution came from SAP Support, they gave us a new API to get Attachments that solved the problem:

https://eu.fsm.cloud.sap/cloud-attachment-service/api/v1/Attachment/${attachment.id}/content?dtos=Attachment.18&account=${account.name}&company=${company.name}

Until now we were using https://eu.coresuite.com/api/data/v4/Attachment/${attachment.id}/content?dtos=Attachment.18&account=${account.name}

Filip

entry.title: Re: How to display invalid entry message in calculation v...
entry.content: As I know, in SAP HANA, the input parameters in calculation views do not have built-in validation.

entry.title: Re: SAP Private linky swear with Azure – running Cloud Connector and SAP Private Link side-by-side
entry.content:
Hey @MofizurRahaman,

there is not much more that can be said about as the blog already states.

1. SAP PLS is no replacement of the SCC. You are comparing a networking service with a software product that includes audit logging, selective RFC exosure etc. They are complementary rather than competitive. Hence, the roadmap item to enable the SCC to communicate with BTP over PLS.

2. SAP PLS supports principal propagation of course, because it is a networking solution. This space has likely evolved since my last post on it but the approach still holds true. See blog part 5.

3. This depends on your own needs. No official guidance published yet.

Let the community know what you decided in the end 🙂

entry.title: SAP integration suite data in excel sheet
entry.content:
Hi experts

Existing approach:

     As we all know, migration from SAP Process Integration/Process Orchestration (PI/PO) to SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI). As part of this process, we have identified the need to generate Excel sheets with some dump data during the assessment phase, which include categories such as “Ready to Migrate,” “Adjustment required,” etc. 


Expecting: Similar type of info(excel sheet) generation for Integration Suite 

    Is it possible/Is there any approach on how to achieve this within the SAP Integration Suite, including any API's/other solutions or scripts that can facilitate this process. We need to generate similar Excel sheets within the SAP Integration Suite.Like Connection URL, User id, Password (Is not expected), any query string, type of connector , etc.Mapping specification if mapping is involved in iflow.We are exploring options to achieve this through API or other solutions.

Could you provide detailed instructions or examples on how to generate Excel sheets from data within the Integration Suite? Specifically, we are looking for advanced methods to automate this process and ensure seamless integration with our existing workflows.

 

Thank you....

entry.title: Re: how to find SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition DB size
entry.content: If the systems are connected into C-ALM, you can configure the Health Monitoring function and check the history of the DB size, for each tenant.

entry.title: Where can I learn the commercial and legal dimensions of SAP Business AI?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring SAP Business AI Legal and Commercial Aspects learning journey.

Overview
The course is designed to provide learners with a thorough understanding of both the commercial and legal dimensions of SAP Business AI.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, learners will possess a comprehensive understanding of the commercial and legal aspects of SAP Business AI. They will be able to identify and explain the fundamental functionalities of SAP Business AI and Joule, understand and navigate the pricing models and commercial implications, and address legal concerns including compliance and ethical deployment of AI within their organizations.

 


Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations on your S
entry.content:
Hi @RoosReijers , It's bit complex due to the nature of individual configuration types. The only possibility is go with 'should we just tell everyone not to make changes and run change audit reports to keep on top of accidental changes and revert them so systems stay aligned'.

But if you need guidance and better possibility then please share the list of configurations where your need more support.

Thanks,

Hari

entry.title: Re: what is the purpose of g-table?
entry.content:

There is a message that is exactly "  It only copies the "G-table" entries of an application component and not the "C-table" content.", so there are some concept that refers to "G-table" and "C-table".

I have the same doubt.

Regards,

Pedro López

entry.title: WBS Element to Control Budgeting(Capex)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Expert,

On ECC 6.0, My client uses internal order for controlling purchase assets(Capex)

Currently, the S/4Hana Public cloud removes the internal order function.

My client requested to control budgeting for purchase Assets (Capex). 

- I use WBS to control the budget, but when I import WBS for budget the system requests input G/L Account.

  Can I import directly to WBS?

- The public cloud support "Overall" planning or not.

Please recommendation.

Thanks,

May

entry.title: Re: Stories, filter down to the last performance and potential ratings
entry.content: I am also looking for this answer. I want to neatly show all performance ratings given to an employee. I can do this, however, I am getting repeating lines of data. My current report is an optimised story report and I have tried both cross tab and non-aggregated lists. My colleague has created a solution in the non-optimised story which works well, however we try not to use the non-optimised model as I know it is due to retire.

entry.title: Re: Convert PNG/JPG attachment to base64 and send it via...
entry.content:
Solution from SAP Support, use new API to get attachment:

https://eu.fsm.cloud.sap/cloud-attachment-service/api/v1/Attachment/$ {attachment.id}/content?dtos=Attachment.18&account=${account.name}&company=${company.name}

instead of the previous version:

https://eu.coresuite.com/api/data/v4/Attachment/{attachment.id}/content?dtos=Attachment.18&account=${account.name}

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Fortaleza 2025
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Fortaleza 2025 – Save the Date!

SAP Inside Track FORTALEZA will have its sixth edition on October 25, 2025.

The event is held in several countries around the world. Its organizers are active members of the SAP Community to share knowledge and strengthen professionals among the participants.
The event has been held in several cities worldwide for over 15 years.

Learn more about how to hold your SAP Inside Track event in your city through this LINK.

We are thrilled to announce the SAP Inside Track Fortaleza edition, held at Beira Mar em Fortaleza on October 25th, 2025!

Following an incredible first edition last year, we’re gearing up for an even more exciting event next year. Get ready for cutting-edge discussions, deep dives into SAP innovations, and hands-on sessions on the most pressing topics in the SAP world.

Key Topics for 2025:

  • Adoption and Migration to S/4 HANA: Strategies, best practices, and lessons learned to make your journey smoother.
  • Instant Payments: How SAP is revolutionizing the payments landscape with faster and more secure transactions.
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML): The future of AI in SAP, from automation to decision-making and beyond.
  • Sustainability and Green Ledger: Sustainability in the SAP ecosystem and the role of digital ledgers in a greener future.
  • Low-Code/No-Code Development: Empowering business users and developer

Join us for an inspiring day filled with insights from industry experts, networking opportunities, and the latest SAP trends that will shape the future of your business. You won’t want to miss it!

Mark your calendars:
Date: October 25th, 2025
Location: Beira Mar em Fortaleza

Stay tuned for more details and registration info coming soon!

Don’t miss out – this is the SAP event of the year in Fortaleza!
#SAPInsideTrack #SAPPorto2025 #S4HANA #InstantPayments #GenAI #GreenLedger #SAPCommunity

Inscription:

Includes:

  • Access to the event;
  • Access to all the lectures;
  • Sponsors for the event here:  
  • Candidates can be received to participate as volunteers in the event, link: 
  • Admissions to the event, briefly the link.

entry.title: how to display fields before alv o/p like header data
entry.content: i wanted to archieve same o/p as fbl3n tcode ...i have separated alv output using sorting and receiving few lists but for each list i want separate header data as u can see in image ...i am not sure if i can use top-of-list event ..if anyone have piece of code for this functionality please share me .i checked few sap sample pograms but it was beyond my understanding

entry.title: Re: ME2L not showing 2 deliveries on same PO
entry.content:
HI,

ME2L tcode is used to display the purchase order. If you want to view the delivery according to the order, you can use the LIPS table. You can see the delivery by typing the order you created in the VGBEL field in the table.

entry.title: Re: how to read fields of structures
entry.content: Hello @mark_fryu , it looks like you got some help. If your question was answered, please accept. Thank you and have a great day!

entry.title: Re: Create SAP S/4HANA External GL Account Hierarchy within SAP Datasphere using standard CDS Views
entry.content:
Hello @JoelleS 

I have succeeded to build the hierarchy successfully. Although I have one issue with the text for the root and inner nodes. Leafs are ok.

My output in the Analytical Model looks as bellow. I tried switching the Presentation between ID and Description etc

I have already done the text association between Dimension View HRF: G/L Account Group (Hierarchy Node) (DIM) and Text View HRF: G/L Account Group (Hierarchy Node) -Text (IL).

Mappings bellow.

Also, I have chosen HierarchyNode as representative key in both views.

Attributes are also set up correct for the Text View HRF: G/L Account Group (Hierarchy Node) -Text (IL) (bellow)

Can you kindly advise if I am missing anything? I have also tried to redeploy all the views and analytical model again thinking it might help somehow.

For some reason it's not showing up correct. The text descriptions are existing in the text view for the respective nodes. I am using the SAP Datasphere trial system extracting the data from the S/4HANA on premise system via replication flow in local tables on top of which I have built the views as advised, using the correct semantic types.

 

Many thanks in advance!

Kind regards!

entry.title: Re: Abap how to display internal table content in the rig...
entry.content: CLASS screen_init DEFINITION. " CREATE PUBLIC. "PRIVATE. PUBLIC SECTION. CLASS-METHODS init_screen. METHODS constructor. PRIVATE SECTION. DATA: splitter_h TYPE REF TO cl_gui_splitter_container, * splitter_v TYPE REF TO cl_gui_splitter_container, picture TYPE REF TO cl_gui_picture, tree TYPE REF TO cl_gui_simple_tree . METHODS: fill_tree, fill_picture. ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Dear Sir Please Add BOM Price in this Sales Report.
entry.content:
SELECT T0.DocEntry,t0.docnum as 'Invoice No', --T0.[U_CustomersGRNNo],T0.[U_GRDate],


M.[BeginStr] as 'Prerflix',T0.[NumAtCard],
(SELECT Top 1 X.[County] FROM CRD1 X Where T0.CardCode = X.CardCode)as'County',
T0.[DocDate],T0.[CANCELED], T0.[TaxDate], T0.[CardName], a.[ItmsGrpCod], a.[U_ItemSubGroup], a.[SalUnitMsr],
(case when t0.gsttrantyp='GA' then 'GST Tax Invoice'
when t0.gsttrantyp='GD' then 'GST Debit Memo'
else 'Bill Of Supply' end ) as 'Document Type',
--(Select N.[U_BPSubGroup] From OCRD N Where T0.CardCode = N.CardCode)as'BP Sub Group',
t3.bpgstn, t0.paytocode as 'Bill To', t0.shiptocode as 'Ship To', T1.[OcrCode],
(Select top 1 T.[Location] from OLCT T where T.Code=t1.LocCode ) as 'Location',
T3.[BpGSTN] as 'BP GSTIN',

(SELECT Top 1 S.[Name] FROM CRD1 R INNER JOIN OCST S ON R.[State] = S.[Code] Where T0.CardCode = R.CardCode )as'State',
T1.[ItemCode], T1.[Dscription],A.[FrgnName],T1.[Quantity],T1.[U_NoOfPkgs], T1.[U_DNo], T1.[U_QtyInKG], l.ItmsGrpNam, T1.[Weight1],T1.[LineTotal]-(T1.LineTotal*T0.[DiscPrcnt]/100) 'Basic Value',

T1.[PriceBefDi],T1.[Price], T0.[RoundDif],T1.[DiscPrcnt],T0.[DocTotal],-- A.[U_ProductGroup], A.[U_SubGroup],
t2.chapterid,---T8.[DistNumber],T8.[MnfDate], T8.[ExpDate],

--(SELECT X.[U_GST] FROM [@UOM] X where X.[Name] = T1.[unitMsr])as'Unit1', T1.[PackQty],
--(SELECT X.[U_GST] FROM [@UOM] X where X.[Name] = a.[SalPackMsr])as'Unit2',T1.[Quantity], t1.price, T1.[LineTotal]-(T1.LineTotal*T0.[DiscPrcnt]/100) 'Basic Value',

(SELECT top 1 ISNULL(T7.stccode,0) FROM OINV T5 INNER JOIN INV1 T6 ON T5.DocEntry=T6.DocEntry INNER JOIN INV4 T7 ON T5.DocEntry=T7.DocEntry Where T7.StaType=-120 AND T6.LineNum=T1.LineNum AND T5.DocEntry=T0.DocEntry AND T6.ItemCode=T1.ItemCode AND T6.LineNum=T7.LineNum)'IGSTRate%',
(SELECT ISNULL(sum(T7.TaxSUm),0) FROM OINV T5 INNER JOIN INV1 T6 ON T5.DocEntry=T6.DocEntry INNER JOIN INV4 T7 ON T5.DocEntry=T7.DocEntry Where T7.StaType=-120 AND T6.LineNum=T1.LineNum AND T5.DocEntry=T0.DocEntry AND T6.ItemCode=T1.ItemCode AND T6.LineNum=T7.LineNum and t7.expnscode=-1 )IGSTAmt,

(SELECT top 1 ISNULL(T7.stccode,0) FROM OINV T5 INNER JOIN INV1 T6 ON T5.DocEntry=T6.DocEntry INNER JOIN INV4 T7 ON T5.DocEntry=T7.DocEntry Where T7.StaType= 8 AND T6.LineNum=T1.LineNum AND T5.DocEntry=T0.DocEntry AND T6.ItemCode=T1.ItemCode AND T6.LineNum=T7.LineNum)'TCSRate%',
(SELECT ISNULL(sum(T7.TaxSUm),0) FROM OINV T5 INNER JOIN INV1 T6 ON T5.DocEntry=T6.DocEntry INNER JOIN INV4 T7 ON T5.DocEntry=T7.DocEntry Where T7.StaType= 8 AND T6.LineNum=T1.LineNum AND T5.DocEntry=T0.DocEntry AND T6.ItemCode=T1.ItemCode AND T6.LineNum=T7.LineNum and t7.expnscode=-1 )TCSAmt,

(SELECT top 1 ISNULL(T7.stccode,0) FROM OINV T5 INNER JOIN INV1 T6 ON T5.DocEntry=T6.DocEntry INNER JOIN INV4 T7 ON T5.DocEntry=T7.DocEntry Where T7.StaType=-100 AND T6.LineNum=T1.LineNum AND T5.DocEntry=T0.DocEntry AND T6.ItemCode=T1.ItemCode AND T6.LineNum=T7.LineNum)'CGSTRate%',
(SELECT ISNULL(sum(T7.TaxSUm),0) FROM OINV T5 INNER JOIN INV1 T6 ON T5.DocEntry=T6.DocEntry INNER JOIN INV4 T7 ON T5.DocEntry=T7.DocEntry Where T7.StaType=-100 AND T6.LineNum=T1.LineNum AND T5.DocEntry=T0.DocEntry AND T6.ItemCode=T1.ItemCode AND T6.LineNum=T7.LineNum and t7.expnscode=-1 )CGSTAmt,

(SELECT top 1 ISNULL(T7.stccode,0) FROM OINV T5 INNER JOIN INV1 T6 ON T5.DocEntry=T6.DocEntry INNER JOIN INV4 T7 ON T5.DocEntry=T7.DocEntry Where T7.StaType=-110 AND T6.LineNum=T1.LineNum AND T5.DocEntry=T0.DocEntry AND T6.ItemCode=T1.ItemCode AND T6.LineNum=T7.LineNum)'SGSTRate%',
(SELECT ISNULL(sum(T7.TaxSUm),0) FROM OINV T5 INNER JOIN INV1 T6 ON T5.DocEntry=T6.DocEntry INNER JOIN INV4 T7 ON T5.DocEntry=T7.DocEntry Where T7.StaType=-110 AND T6.LineNum=T1.LineNum AND T5.DocEntry=T0.DocEntry AND T6.ItemCode=T1.ItemCode AND T6.LineNum=T7.LineNum and t7.expnscode=-1 )SGSTAmt,

 

(case when t1.VisOrder=0 then
(select sum(distinct t.basesum) from inv4 t where t.expnscode in (1,2,3,4) and t.docentry=t0.docentry)
when t1.VisOrder>=1 then 0 end) as 'Freight Amt',

(case when t1.VisOrder=0 then
(select sum( t.taxsum) from inv4 t where t.expnscode in (1,2,3,4) and t.docentry=t0.docentry)
when t1.VisOrder>=1 then 0 end) as 'Freight Tax Amt',

(case when t1.VisOrder=0 then
(select top 1 t.stccode from inv4 t where t.expnscode in (1,2,3,4) and t.docentry=t0.docentry)
when t1.VisOrder>=1 then '' end) as 'Freight Tax Rate',


T1.[LineTotal],H.[SlpName],T1.[AcctCode],T0.[U_VehicleNo], T0.[U_Destination],T0.[U_Transporter],T0.[U_LRNo], T0.[U_LRDate], T0.[U_Ewaybill], T0.[U_TransporterGST]

 

 


FROM dbo.OINV T0
INNER JOIN dbo.INV1 T1 ON T0.[DocEntry] = T1.[DocEntry]
INNER JOIN NNM1 M ON T0.[Series] = M.[Series]
LEFT JOIN dbo.OITM a ON T1.[ItemCode] = a.[ItemCode]
LEFT JOIN dbo.OITB l on a.ItmsGrpCod = l.ItmsGrpCod
LEFT JOIN dbo.OCHP T2 ON T1.HsnEntry = T2.[AbsEntry]
LEFT JOIN dbo.inv12 t3 on t0.docentry=t3.docentry
INNER JOIN dbo.OLCT T4 ON T1.[LocCode] = T4.[Code]
LEFT JOIN DLN1 T5 ON T1.BaseEntry = T5.DocEntry And T5.BaseLine = T1.LineNum AND T1.WhsCode=T5.WhsCode AND T1.ItemCode=T5.ItemCode
LEFT JOIN ODLN T6 ON T5.DocEntry = T6.DocEntry
INNER JOIN OSLP H ON T0.[SlpCode] = H.[SlpCode]
--left outer join IBT1 I1 on T1.ItemCode=I1.ItemCode and (T5.DocEntry=I1.BaseEntry and T5.ObjType=I1.BaseType)
--left outer join OBTN T8 on T8.ItemCode=I1.ItemCode and I1.BatchNum=T8.DistNumber
LEFT JOIN WTR1 T16 On T16.DocEntry=T1.BaseEntry and T16.LineNum=T1.BaseLine
--left outer join IBT1 I11 on T1.ItemCode=I11.ItemCode and (T16.DocEntry=I11.BaseEntry and T16.ObjType=I11.BaseType)
--left outer join OBTN T18 on T18.ItemCode=I11.ItemCode and I11.BatchNum=T18.DistNumber


WHERE T4.[GSTRegnNo] =[%2] and T0.[DocDate] >=[%0] AND T0.[DocDate] <=[%1] and T0.Canceled = 'N' AND T0.GSTTranTyp NOT IN('GD')

ORDER BY T0.[DocDate],T0.[DocNum]

entry.title: For standard Subcontracting Process, how to link Subcon PO with it's corresponding Subcon Challan?
Re: SAP B1 Alerts is not working.
entry.content:
Hi Krish_SAP

Please troubleshot the issue with help of below guided answer :

https://ga.support.sap.com/dtp/viewer/index.html#/tree/2977/actions/44405

Still having issue,please log incident using component "SBO-BC-JOB"

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Alerts are not sending

Hi Krish_SAP

Please troubleshot the issue with help of below guided answer :

https://ga.support.sap.com/dtp/viewer/index.html#/tree/2977/actions/44405

Still having issue,please log incident using component "SBO-BC-JOB"

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition joins forces with Microsoft Sentinel!
entry.content:
Dear SAP and Microsoft community,

We are thrilled to bring you an exciting update from the world of SAP security. We are proud to announce the release of a native integration between SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition, and Microsoft Sentinel. This collaboration marks a significant milestone in our ongoing efforts to offer our joint customers the best of both worlds in protecting against cyber attacks.

 

Overview of SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition and Microsoft Sentinel Integration

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition, is a combined SAP-provided SaaS solution and security managed service, specifically designed to monitor SAP applications for suspicious activities and abnormal user behavior. It offers robust defense through comprehensive log data analysis and correlation to proactively identify potential threats. Tailored for SAP software, it uses attack detection patterns and forensic capabilities to counter known and evolving threats. Its seamless integration with third-party systems ensures comprehensive security for digital operations.

By teaming up with Microsoft Sentinel, a leading cloud-native security information and event management (SIEM) solution, customers will benefit from enhanced security insights and comprehensive threat detection across their entire IT landscape.

Microsoft Sentinel, a key component of Microsoft’s unified security operations platform, is setting new standards in cybersecurity innovation. It uses AI and automation to detect, investigate, and respond to threats swiftly. Microsoft Sentinel powers capabilities such as advanced threat hunting and security signal correlation enriched with SAP insights, offering a cutting-edge solution to protect critical business data.

"By integrating SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition with Microsoft Sentinel, we are providing our customers with a powerful solution to safeguard their critical assets," said Michael Schmitt, Product Lead at SAP. "This partnership underscores our commitment to delivering innovative security solutions that address the complex challenges faced by modern enterprises."

The combined solution will offer

  1. Enhanced Security Insights: By combining SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition with Microsoft Sentinel, customers gain a more comprehensive view of their IT landscape, enabling deeper visibility into potential threats and vulnerabilities.
  2. Leading Automated Response: Using Microsoft Sentinel’s leading SOAR capabilities, Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition may contribute to fully automated attack disruption or semi-automated SAP user blocking via Microsoft Teams.
  3. Proactive Threat Identification: The integration leverages advanced threat detection patterns and forensic capabilities from both SAP and Microsoft to proactively identify and mitigate sophisticated cyber threats before they can impact critical SAP applications.
  4. Unified Threat Management across IT Landscape: This native integration allows for seamless analysis and correlation of security data from SAP systems within the Microsoft Sentinel platform, streamlining threat detection and response processes.
  5. Improved Efficiency and Collaboration: The partnership enables SAP security teams with general security teams to work more efficiently by centralizing threat monitoring and response activities, thus reducing the complexity and time required to manage diverse security tools.

This collaboration not only enhances the security posture of joint SAP and Microsoft customers but also represents our commitment to innovation and the delivery of cutting-edge security solutions for modern enterprises.

"We are thrilled to partner with SAP to complement our SAP security portfolio for our joint customers," said Yossi Hasson, Product Lead at OneSOC at Microsoft. "This collaboration will enable organizations to leverage the strengths of both SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition and Microsoft Sentinel, ensuring they stay ahead of emerging threats."

What’s next?

  • Additional out-of-the-box detections across SAP and Microsoft
  • Updates to the SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition documentation and roadmaps regarding Sentinel
  • Webinar showcasing the scenario and further comments on the partnership

Final Words

The collaboration leveraging the cloud-native capabilities of Microsoft Sentinel alongside SAP Enterprise Threat Detection ensures that security measures can scale with growing business needs and adapt to evolving cyber threats.

This new integration will be available to customers and partners starting Q4 2024. Sign-Up from this Form.

Follow the instructions given and you are good to go. See you there.

This blog was co-authored by Yossi Hasson (Sentinel Product Lead, Microsoft), Martin Pankraz (SAP PM, Microsoft), Michael Schmitt (Product Lead ETD Cloud, SAP) Arndt Lingscheid (Global Solution Owner Cyber Security and Data Protection, SAP)

 

Cheers

Michael, Yossi, Martin, and Arndt

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS to UI5 Smart table. Annotations issue
entry.content:
Hi, 

you must specify the annotation model when creating the ODataModel.

new ODataModel("/sap/opu/odata/yourServiceName",
                {               annotationURI: "/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/catalogservice;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='ANNOTATION_NAME',Version='0001')/$value/"
 
ANNOTATION_NAME: You find this name in the SEGW->RuntimeFile (Ends with 'ANNO_MDL').

entry.title: Not able to change profit center from 102 to 101 in sales order (VA02)
entry.content:
Hello,

I am facing one issue, where i am not able to change profit center from 102 to 101 in sales order (VA02), it is giving a prompt like "Profit center set to 102". Any one faced similar issue.

entry.title: Re: OData Versioning
entry.content: How does this work if we use CDS / BOPF generated OData Services? Where and how can I maintain the version in such cases?

entry.title: Re: ME2L not showing 2 deliveries on same PO
entry.content: Tcode SE16N

entry.title: Re: Export Scheme , Import Scheme button not seen into SL...

Hi nikunjmehta2290 

Please check if issue is same as mentioned in note   2643612 

Than You.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Massive Activation of BP Credit management
entry.content:
Hi all, 

in S/4HANA which is the official tool for upload credit data, when you want to activate credit management for thousands of customers?

I have to create UKM000 role and the related credit segments for all the customers, with different credit limits. 

I've recorded a LSMW, but it' s too slow....

What is the recomended tool?

It would be better to be able to work on it by sapgui, but also a fiori app can be useful.

entry.title: Re: Changing a bulk of the BP (Customer) Group, is that p...
entry.content: our number range (VS1******) automatically, Applied for all custoumers regardless of whichever group they belong to.

entry.title: Re: http status 404 not found when login SLD
entry.content:
Hi kkphan

The reported scenario seems the same we have documented in attached SAP NOTE 3149311 - Error "HTTP Status 404 - Not Found" When SLD Is Using Port 30010.
Please, perform the referred workaround and let us know the results.

For the HTTP Not found 404 --The server has not found anything matching the URI given; page not found, or file not found, we can try below steps,
1.           Restart your browser. Try closing the current window and opening a new one.
2.           Clear cache. Remove your browser data and history. 
3.           Use another device. 
4.           Switch to the Incognito window.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: how to Get logged in User details in custom SAP FIORI from ECC
entry.content: not on FLP yet , i run it local in BAS

entry.title: Re: How to scrape images from Google
entry.content:
One thing I forgot to mention. When you are recording your screen, for example with a search term "cat", the automation will set the criteria for recognizes the screen by the word "cat" being in the title.

You have to change his, for example, to contains "Google Search".
